title: Citrusy Jamaican Potato Starch Skewers
description: A light, juicy jerk‑flavored chicken skewer coated with potato starch for a crisp finish, served hot from the air fryer and packed with citrus zest for a bright finish.
instructions: In a bowl combine the chicken cubes, salt, pepper, smoked paprika, allspice, garlic powder, and Jamaican jerk seasoning.
Add the potato starch and mix until all chicken pieces are evenly coated.
Stir in the fresh lime juice, orange juice, and olive oil, ensuring the chicken is well mixed.
Allow the mixture to marinate for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Thread the marinated chicken cubes onto metal skewers, packing 3‑4 pieces per skewer and adding some diced red bell pepper for color.
Preheat the air fryer to 200 °C (400 °F) for 3 minutes.
Place the skewers in a single layer in the air fryer basket and cook for 10‑12 minutes, flipping halfway, until the chicken is golden and reaches an internal temperature of 75 °C (165 °F).
Remove the skewers, garnish with chopped cilantro if desired, and serve hot.
Enjoy the citrusy, spicy snap of each bite.
